# Dentic

> Dentic is a scientific tooth brushing app that guides users through the Bass Method with 3D oral navigation, a segmented timer, and daily habit streaks.

## About

Dentic helps people brush their teeth correctly using the clinically recommended Bass Method. It provides real-time guidance through every stage of a brushing session, ensuring no tooth surface is missed.

The app is designed as a mobile-first web application, planned for packaging as a native app.

## Core Features

- **3D Oral Navigation**: An interactive 3D tooth model that highlights which zone to brush next in real time
- **Segmented Timer**: Automatically advances through 7 brushing zones with audio and haptic cues; default session is 3 minutes
- **Daily Check-in Streaks**: AM/PM logging with a monthly calendar view and quality scoring
- **Bass Method Guidance**: Step-by-step coaching based on the most effective clinically proven tooth brushing technique
- **Toothbrush Mode**: Supports both manual and electric toothbrush workflows

## Target Users

- People who want to build a consistent, scientifically grounded brushing habit
- Individuals advised by dentists to improve their brushing technique
- Users seeking guidance on the Bass Method (also called the Modified Bass Technique)

## Key Concepts

**Bass Method**: A tooth brushing technique recommended by dental professionals. The brush is held at a 45° angle to the tooth surface with bristles directed toward the gumline. Short horizontal vibrating strokes (approximately 2mm) are applied to 2–3 teeth at a time, followed by a sweeping motion toward the crown. The technique systematically covers all labial, lingual, and occlusal surfaces. Each session should last no less than 3 minutes.

**3D Oral Navigation**: A visual map of the mouth divided into brushing zones. The app tracks coverage and prompts the user when to move to the next zone.

**Brushing Zones**: The mouth is divided into segments — upper right, upper front, upper left, lower right, lower front, lower left — covering both outer (labial/buccal) and inner (lingual/palatal) surfaces, plus occlusal surfaces.

## Technology

- Platform: Web (mobile-first), planned iOS and Android native packaging
- Pricing: Free
